description: Rebuild the Assets list page and Asset detail page from the frontend-design/ V1 wireframes, scoped to what the current backend supports. Seed a realistic DC-12 Hudson Valley demo project so all pages have data to render. See scripts/ralph/prd.md for full rationale, decisions, and out-of-scope items.

userStories.title: Backend — add assets.vendor_name TEXT column
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want assets to have a vendor_name TEXT column so I can display vendor info on the asset list and detail pages without standing up an Organizations CRUD UI first. CONTEXT.md says vendor is properly an Organization; this column is an explicit temporary denormalized snapshot.
acceptanceCriteria: Create migrations/020_asset_vendor_name.sql containing `ALTER TABLE assets ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS vendor_name TEXT;`
Create backend/apply_vendor_name_migration.py mirroring the existing apply_*_migration.py pattern (psycopg2 + dotenv)
Update backend/contexts/asset_registry/assets.py: add `vendor_name` to the Asset response model, accept it in create/update payloads, include in INSERT and UPDATE SQL
Update backend/contexts/asset_registry/api.py: serialize vendor_name in _serialize
Update backend/contexts/asset_registry/test_assets.py: at least one test creates an asset with vendor_name and asserts it round-trips through GET
Run apply script — column appears in assets table
Existing backend tests pass

userStories.title: Backend — idempotent seed script for DC-12 Hudson Valley demo project
userStories.description: As a developer testing the frontend locally, I want a single command that seeds a realistic demo project bound to my logged-in user so every page renders meaningful data instead of 'Failed to load templates'.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/seed_demo_data.py — standalone Python, psycopg2 + dotenv (loads ../.env.local)
Accept --email flag (default: SEED_USER_EMAIL env or 'shlokp98@gmail.com'); resolve to auth.users.id; error loudly if not found
Idempotent: DELETE prior project named 'DC-12 Hudson Valley (demo)' (cascades) before inserting
Insert: 1 Org ('Hudson DC LLC (demo)'), 1 Project, 4 DisciplineScopes (Mechanical/Electrical/Controls/General Construction), 1 participation row for the user
Spaces tree: Building → 3 Halls (A/B/C) → 3 Rows under Hall B
5 AssetTypes: AHU, PDU, UPS, CRAH, Switchgear
≥15 Assets across the halls with realistic tags/mfr/model/serial/vendor_name/nameplate_data, ≥2 parent/child relationships (e.g., Switchgear → PDU → UPS)
2 Systems with asset memberships ('Cooling — Chilled Water', 'Power — Hall B')
5 TestProcedureTemplates (mix of L2/L3/L5) linked to relevant AssetTypes via asset_type_template_links
≥12 TestProcedureInstances across assets with varied statuses (pending/in_progress/complete) so phase derivation has signal
Script prints the resulting project_id + a clickable localhost URL
Running the script twice succeeds (idempotent)

userStories.title: Frontend — port wireframe primitives into frontend/src/lib/frontend-kit/
userStories.description: As a developer building screens from the frontend-design/ wireframes, I want the W* primitives (WBox, WPill, WT, etc.) available as proper TypeScript components so I can compose screens with the same density and language as the wireframes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend/src/lib/frontend-kit/ with TypeScript ports of: WBox, WPill, WT, WH, WAvatar, WBar, WStamp, WIcon, WLiveDot, WSectionLabel, WBtn, WHeader, WSideNav, WFrame
Source of truth: frontend-design/wireframe-kit.jsx (read carefully — port semantics, not just markup)
All CSS classes namespaced fk-* so they don't collide with existing bp-* styles
CSS lives in frontend/src/lib/frontend-kit/styles.css, imported once from layout.tsx
Each component has prop types; no `any`
No `Object.assign(window, ...)` — proper ES module exports
Add a Storybook-style scratch route /frontend-kit-preview that renders one of each primitive for visual QA (can be deleted after sign-off; mark with a // SCRATCH comment)
Existing pages unaffected — bundle check: no regression in /assets page render

userStories.title: Frontend — Assets list page V1 dense-table rebuild
userStories.description: As a user landing on /project/[id]/assets, I want a dense, scannable table of assets with filter chips so I can find the asset I'm looking for quickly. Matches V1 of frontend-design/screens/equipment.jsx.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite frontend/src/app/(app)/project/[id]/assets/page.tsx using frontend-kit primitives

Layout: header crumbs, decorative AI command bar (WWell + ✦ glyph; input is non-functional, shows 'coming soon' on submit), action pills (+ new opens create modal from US-007; import BIM + export are decorative stubs), filter chip row, table
Table columns: ☐ (decorative — no bulk select), Tag · description, AssetType, Space path (e.g. 'Hall B / Row 3'), Status (lifecycle pill), Phase (derived per US-006), Children count, Vendor
Phase rendering: 'Pre-install' when no instances; 'L2 / Pre-functional', 'L3 / Functional', 'L4 / Functional Performance', 'L5 / IST' otherwise (highest in_progress or complete)
Filter chips wired to existing useUrlFilters hook: space, asset_type, lifecycle status. Chip click toggles filter; X clears it
Live row count chip ('X of Y · live') with WLiveDot
Row click → navigate to /project/[id]/assets/[assetId]
Empty state: friendly empty box, no 'Failed to load' string ever shown
Loading state: skeleton rows, not a centered spinner
Delete frontend/src/contexts/asset_registry/ui.tsx (the old AssetList component) and any references
Delete frontend/src/contexts/asset_registry/ui.test.tsx
Write new tests: filter chip toggle updates URL, row click navigates, derived phase renders correctly for an asset with N instances

userStories.title: Frontend — Asset detail page rebuild with 5 tabs
userStories.description: As a user clicking into an asset, I want a rich detail page showing everything we know about the asset (hero + KPIs + tabbed sections). Matches frontend-design/screens/asset-detail.jsx, trimmed to what the backend supports.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite frontend/src/app/(app)/project/[id]/assets/[assetId]/page.tsx using frontend-kit primitives

Hero: WStamps row (tag, type, disc derived from space hierarchy if any, loc=space path, asset id), big WH tag + name, lifecycle status pill (active/retired/decommissioned), vendor pill, scheduled date pill IF nameplate_data has one (else omit)
KPI strip (5 boxes): Cx progress % (per US-006), sub-asset count, instances done/total, manufacturer, model. (Drops 'submittals' and 'turnover' from design — no backend.)
Tab strip: Overview, Devices, Checklists, Tests, Linked — exactly these 5; Issues, Files, RFIs, History are NOT rendered
Active tab persisted in URL query (?tab=devices)
Overview panel: KV grid of all asset fields including all nameplate_data keys; activity timeline = empty state ('No activity log yet')
Devices panel: list of child assets (assets with parent_asset_id = this asset); click → navigate to that asset's detail
Checklists panel: list of TestProcedureInstances where level=L2 for this asset; show template name, status, created_at
Tests panel: list of TestProcedureInstances where level in (L3,L4,L5) for this asset; same columns
Linked panel: parent asset (1 row, if any) + system memberships (N rows) + space path breadcrumb
Loading + empty states for each panel; no 'Failed to load' strings
Tests for: hero renders all available fields, tab switching updates URL, each panel makes the right API call

userStories.title: Frontend — phase derivation helper + lifecycle phase tracker visual
userStories.description: As both the list and detail pages need to show 'phase', I want a single shared derivation function and a PhaseTracker component matching the design.
acceptanceCriteria: Add frontend/src/contexts/asset_registry/derivePhase.ts: function takes TestProcedureInstance[] and returns one of: 'pre-install' | 'L2' | 'L3' | 'L4' | 'L5'
Rule (per PRD decision #4): highest level among instances where status in ('in_progress','complete'); 'pre-install' if no instances
Unit tests: empty array → pre-install; one L2 pending → pre-install; one L2 in_progress → L2; L2 complete + L3 pending → L2; L2 complete + L3 in_progress → L3; L4 complete + L2 in_progress → L4
Add PhaseTracker component in frontend-kit: 8 phase circles (Design, Submittals, Install, Pre-functional, Functional, IST, Turnover, Operations) with done/current/upcoming states
Map derived phase → tracker state: pre-install=current at Install; L2=current at Pre-functional; L3/L4=current at Functional; L5=current at IST
Used by the detail page hero area

userStories.title: Frontend — Create-asset modal
userStories.description: As a user, I want a 'New asset' button on the list page that opens a modal form so I can create assets without leaving the page.
acceptanceCriteria: Modal triggered by the '+ new' pill on the list page

Form fields: tag (required, unique-per-project hint), name, asset_type (select, populated from /asset-types endpoint), space (cascading select: Building → Hall → child), parent_asset (optional search/select), manufacturer, model, serial, vendor_name, nameplate_data (key-value JSON editor with add/remove rows)
Submit → POST /projects/{id}/assets; on success: close modal, optimistic-insert into list, toast success
Server validation errors surfaced inline next to the offending field (especially 409 tag-conflict)
Cancel button + Esc + click-outside all close the modal without saving
Tests: form submits with valid data, displays 409 error, cascading space select narrows correctly

userStories.title: Frontend — Asset edit + delete affordances
userStories.description: As a user on the asset detail page, I want to edit any editable field inline and delete the asset (with confirmation).
acceptanceCriteria: Detail hero: each editable field (name, manufacturer, model, serial, vendor_name) becomes an inline-edit input on click; Enter saves, Esc cancels
Nameplate_data: edit via 'Edit nameplate' button that opens the same JSON KV editor from US-007
Save → PATCH /projects/{id}/assets/{assetId}; optimistic update, rollback on error
Delete button in hero overflow menu → confirmation dialog naming the asset → DELETE /projects/{id}/assets/{assetId} → navigate back to list
Retire + Decommission actions also exposed in the overflow menu (POST .../retire and .../decommission)
Tests: inline edit round-trips, delete confirmation cancels safely, retire updates the status pill

userStories.title: Cleanup — purge old asset UI, complete test coverage, smoke verify
userStories.description: Final pass to ensure no remnants of the old setup remain and the whole flow works end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Grep confirms no remaining import of frontend/src/contexts/asset_registry/ui (the deleted module)
Grep confirms no occurrence of the string 'Failed to load templates' anywhere in the assets page render path
Run frontend test suite: all pass
Run backend test suite: all pass

Manual smoke: fresh DB, run migration + seed, log in, navigate to /project/<id>/assets, click an asset, switch tabs, edit a field, create a new asset, delete it — every step works without console errors
